evidence: The paper uses Gini impurity for feature importance ranking (Eq. 6-7, lines 476-481) and interprets magnitude/ranking when discussing ATP6V1C2 as "especially important for survival prediction" (line 1106). However, this ranking is corroborated by TreeSHAP (lines 577-603, Supplementary Fig. 9 validates ATP6V1C2 via SHAP), held-out test validation (80-20 split in Tables 2-4), and GO enrichment (p<0.001 biological validation in Tables 5-6). Critically, the paper's main conclusions center on module detection and predictive performance, not individual gene rankings. As stated: "feature selection based on features organized in modules may perform superior on unseen test data" (lines 1100-1102). Multiple comparison methods (GNN-SubNet, RF, Boruta) show "similar accuracy" (Table 5), suggesting the module structure matters more than Gini ranking precision.
